skill.name: chronicle-context-retriever
skill.description: Search past development sessions in Chronicle to recover decisions, blockers, implementation details, and related work using MCP tools or Chronicle CLI commands.
skill.summary: Retrieve prior development context from Chronicle sessions and timelines.
skill.author: ChandlerHardy

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged many markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution, plus path traversal, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found no executable script, malicious prompt injection, network exfiltration, or crypto use in SKILL.md, but the skill intentionally guides agents to query local Chronicle history and optionally run Chronicle CLI commands, which can expose sensitive development context if used without user intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Development History Retrieval
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to search Chronicle sessions, summaries, timelines, commits, and optional Obsidian notes. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it may bring private implementation details, repository paths, decisions, blockers, and session history into the active model context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data-access behavior is explicitly documented and central to the skill. It is not malicious by itself, but the privacy impact is real because it retrieves prior local development records.

medium_findings.title: CLI Command Guidance Requires Careful Argument Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Chronicle CLI command examples for searching sessions and commits. The file does not contain executable code or direct shell interpolation, but assistants using these examples should keep user search text quoted and avoid constructing unsafe shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documentation examples, so no direct injection path is present in the skill file. The concern is operational because user-controlled search terms may later be passed to a local CLI.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown code spans, fenced examples, MCP tool names, or Chronicle CLI examples. SKILL.md is documentation only and contains no Ruby code, no backtick-based execution, and no script body.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows markdown documentation and examples rather than executable Ruby syntax. The repeated pattern comes from backticks used for formatting commands and tool names.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Repository Path Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal finding points to an example repo_path value using an ellipsis in a user directory path. It is not filesystem traversal logic, and the skill does not read arbitrary paths directly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a documentation example for filtering Chronicle sessions by repository path. No path normalization, file read, or traversal sequence handling exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Finding Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to the frontmatter description. That line describes searching Chronicle data and contains no hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain text metadata. I found no evidence of cryptographic operations in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are User-Facing Search Phrases
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are natural-language trigger examples and response-format text for recalling past development work. They do not instruct host enumeration, credential discovery, or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is about searching prior Chronicle sessions and avoiding past development mistakes. No system reconnaissance commands or collection logic were found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local CLI Invocation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents Chronicle CLI commands that an assistant may run locally. These examples are expected for the skill, but marketplace users should understand that command execution depends on the local Chronicle installation and user intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI examples are clearly present and relevant. They are not malicious, but they represent a real external command pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Private Context Expansion Through MCP Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends MCP calls that retrieve sessions, summaries, timelines, and commits. This can expand the assistant context with local project history that may include sensitive details.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MCP retrieval workflow is explicit in the documentation. The risk is privacy exposure from retrieved content, not malicious code in the skill.

content.value_statement: Developers lose time rediscovering decisions, blockers, and implementation details from earlier work. This skill searches Chronicle sessions and timelines so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can bring relevant history into the current task.
actual_capabilities: Search Chronicle session summaries and prompts by keyword.
Retrieve detailed session summaries for selected session IDs.
List sessions by time period, repository, or AI tool.
Combine session timelines with related commit history.
Use Chronicle CLI commands when MCP tools are unavailable.
Format findings with session IDs, dates, decisions, outcomes, and relevance.
limitations: Requires Chronicle MCP tools or the Chronicle CLI to be installed and configured.
Search quality depends on the completeness of stored Chronicle session data.
Retrieved history may include sensitive local project context.
Does not create, edit, or repair Chronicle records.

use_cases.title: Recall Prior Implementation Decisions
use_cases.description: Find how a feature was built before and summarize the approach, rationale, and result.

use_cases.title: Recover Blocker History
use_cases.description: Search earlier sessions for known blockers, failed approaches, and fixes before starting related work.

use_cases.title: Build Project Continuity
use_cases.description: Review recent sessions and commits to resume work with accurate context after a break.

prompt_templates.title: Find Recent Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for a recent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: What did I work on yesterday in Chronicle? Summarize the main sessions and outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Recall a Feature Approach
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused topic search across prior sessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Chronicle for previous authentication work. Tell me the implementation approach, key decisions, and related sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Past Attempts
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of successful and blocked approaches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find sessions about database migrations. Separate successful approaches from blockers and explain what applies to the current task.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Context Across Sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Chronicle to trace the user-dashboard feature across sessions and commits. Provide a chronological summary with decisions, outcomes, and open risks.

output_examples.input: How did I implement authentication last time?
output_examples.output: A concise summary of matching sessions, the authentication approach used, key decisions, outcomes, and the session IDs to inspect next.

output_examples.input: What was the blocker with the database migration?
output_examples.output: A focused blocker report that identifies the relevant session, explains the failure mode, lists the resolution, and notes what to avoid now.

output_examples.input: Show all work on the user-dashboard feature.
output_examples.output: A chronological overview of related sessions and commits, with progress milestones, unresolved questions, and links to session references.

best_practices: Start with broad Chronicle searches, then narrow results with exact terms or session IDs.
Review multiple related sessions before presenting a conclusion.
Confirm with the user before surfacing sensitive or unrelated project history.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a single search result tells the full project history.
Do not run Chronicle CLI commands when MCP tools already provide structured results.
Do not include private session details that are unrelated to the user request.

faq.question: What does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches Chronicle sessions, summaries, timelines, and related commit records when those data sources are available.

faq.question: Does it require MCP?
faq.answer: No. MCP is preferred for structured results, but the skill also documents Chronicle CLI fallback commands.

faq.question: Can it search by repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes repository filtering through Chronicle session queries when Chronicle supports that context.

faq.question: Can it recover exact code from old sessions?
faq.answer: It can summarize stored session context, but exact code depends on what Chronicle captured.

faq.question: Is private project history exposed?
faq.answer: It may be included in the assistant context when relevant. Users should request only the history they need.

faq.question: Does it modify my files or Chronicle database?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes search and retrieval workflows, not write operations.
